data.title: Bureau Veritas acquires three new companies in Cybersecurity, Nuclear, and Transition Services to expand its services and portfolios.
data.description: A leading company in Testing, Inspection, and Certification services, Bureau Veritas, has recently made significant moves to expand its portfolio by acquiring three companies specializing in Cybersecurity, Nuclear, and Transition Services. The acquisitions aim to enhance the company's presence in these rapidly growing sectors. In Germany, Bureau Veritas has finalized an agreement with Dornier Group to purchase its subsidiary, Dornier Hinneburg, bolstering its capabilities in the nuclear industry. Additionally, it has also entered into agreements for acquiring the Institute For Cyber. These strategic acquisitions demonstrate Bureau Veritas’ commitment to strategic growth and diversification.

data.title: INJ price at $13.31, -1.48% in 24h, holds $13.31 support after breaking downtrend, fueled by 14% surge on EVM testnet & partnerships.
data.description: Shares of INJ are currently trading at $13.31, representing a decrease of 1.48% over the past 24 hours. This drop comes after a significant 14% increase the previous week, which was fueled by the launch of EVM testnet and key partnerships. The current trading price indicates that investors are still showing strong support for the stock, with bulls defending crucial support levels.

data.title: Crypto market sees retreat as record liquidity and signs of froth emerge. Traders withdraw as positions unwind and profit-taking rises.
data.description: Trading activity has slowed down as traders reduce leveraged positions and take profits, even with record high levels of liquidity in the market. This is happening across major tokens, leading to a decline in trading volumes and increased caution among market participants.

data.title: Cryptocurrency dive: Bitcoin drops under $116K as 213,729 traders face liquidation, losing about $600M in 24 hours.
data.description: Within the last 24 hours, the cryptocurrency market experienced an abrupt downturn resulting in the liquidation of 213,729 traders. The total amount lost by these traders amounted to almost $600 million.

data.title: Voltage CEO predicts Lightning Network to attract 5% of stablecoin flows, capture billions by 2028.
data.description: Graham Krizek, the creator and chief executive officer of Voltage, foresees significant growth for the Lightning Network in the coming years. He anticipates that this technology will secure billions of dollars in worldwide stablecoin transactions in the near future.

data.title: 15% more U.S. exhibitors participate in 2025 CISCE in Beijing, showcasing enhanced supply chain collaboration.
data.description: The recent third China International Supply Chain Expo (CISCE), held in Beijing from July 16 to 20, 2025, served as a crucial hub for worldwide networking and collaboration in supply chain advancement. This event saw a notable 15% rise in the participation of U.S. representatives, reflecting the growing interest and engagement in global supply chain initiatives.

data.title: Experts discuss how Yuhang District in Hangzhou City, China, achieved common prosperity through grassroots governance.
data.description: In Beijing on July 25, 2025, a gathering of economic development and social governance experts discussed the successful promotion of common prosperity in Yuhang District, Hangzhou City, Zhejiang Province, China. The district's innovative grassroots strategies were analyzed by the group to understand how it achieved significant progress in this area.
